name: Lady of the Lake
description: The Lady of the Lake is a powerful enchantress and a pivotal figure in Arthurian legend. Often depicted as a mysterious and benevolent being, she resides in a shimmering lake, her origins shrouded in magic and mystery. She is best known for gifting King Arthur the legendary sword Excalibur, bestowing upon him not only the weapon but also the magical scabbard that protected him from harm. She is also credited with raising Lancelot after the death of his father, training him in chivalry and the arts of combat. Her wisdom and magical abilities make her a valuable advisor to Arthur and a protector of Camelot. Some accounts portray her as Vivienne, a complex figure with both benevolent and manipulative tendencies, while others present her as Nimue, a more purely virtuous being. Regardless of the specific portrayal, the Lady of the Lake embodies the mystical and enchanting aspects of Arthurian lore, representing the power of nature, magic, and feminine wisdom. She often intervenes in the affairs of mortals, guiding heroes, bestowing gifts, and ultimately shaping the destiny of Camelot. Her connection to the sacred waters symbolizes purity, transformation, and the hidden depths of knowledge. She is a guardian of ancient secrets and a powerful force to be reckoned with, her influence felt throughout the Arthurian cycle.
subtitle: Guardian of Excalibur and Enchantress

keyMyths.title: The Gift of Excalibur
keyMyths.description: The Lady of the Lake presents King Arthur with Excalibur, a magical sword, and its scabbard, signifying his rightful claim to the throne. The sword is a symbol of power and justice.

keyMyths.title: Raising Lancelot
keyMyths.description: After the death of his father, the Lady of the Lake raises Lancelot in her underwater kingdom, teaching him chivalry and combat skills. This ensures his loyalty to Arthur.

keyMyths.title: Merlin's Imprisonment
keyMyths.description: In some versions, the Lady of the Lake, often as Nimue or Vivienne, uses her magic to trap Merlin, either out of fear or a desire to control his immense power. This highlights her magical prowess.

sources.author: Sir Thomas Malory
sources.description: A compilation of Arthurian legends, providing a comprehensive account of the Lady of the Lake's role.
